sections.heading: PART II Statement of Policy
sections.title: Policy statement for cybersecurity in banking sector
sections.summary: This section outlines the purpose and scope of the Guidance Note on Cybersecurity for the Banking Sector. It establishes minimum standards for cybersecurity governance and risk management frameworks that institutions must adopt. The Guidance Note aims to create a secure cyberspace, combat cybercrime, protect critical information infrastructure, and maintain public trust in the financial system without replacing existing regulatory obligations.
sections.dtia_summary: This section sets the foundational policy for cybersecurity governance in banking institutions, requiring robust oversight mechanisms and risk management frameworks that must be integrated into the institution's overall risk management strategy. It establishes the baseline for assessing cyber risks and ensuring alignment with regulatory expectations.

requirements.text: Create a safer and more secure cyberspace that underpins information system security priorities and promote stability of the Kenyan banking sector
Establish a coordinated approach to the prevention and combating of cybercrime
Up-scaling of identification and protection of critical information infrastructure
Promotion of compliance with appropriate technical and operational cybersecurity standards
Development of requisite skills, continuous building of capacity and promote a culture of fostering a strong interplay between policy, leveraging on technology to do business and risk management
Maintenance of public trust and confidence in the financial system

sections.source_quote: This Guidance Note outlines the minimum requirements that institutions shall build upon in the development and implementation of strategies, policies, procedures and related activities aimed at mitigating cyber risk.

sections.heading: PART III Specific Requirements
sections.title: Specific cybersecurity requirements for banking institutions
sections.summary: This section details specific cybersecurity requirements including governance structures, regular independent assessments, outsourcing protocols, and training programs. It mandates that institutions establish robust cybersecurity frameworks with clear responsibilities across the board, conduct annual independent threat assessments, and ensure third-party service providers meet regulatory standards. The requirements cover both technical and organizational aspects of cybersecurity.
sections.dtia_summary: This section provides detailed technical and organizational requirements for cybersecurity, including mandatory annual threat assessments, clear governance structures, and third-party risk management protocols that directly impact data transfer and processing security across borders.

requirements.text: Board of Directors should understand the nature of their institution's business and cyber threats
Board should oversee cultivation of ethical governance and management culture
Board should engage management in establishing cybersecurity vision and risk appetite
Board should allocate adequate cybersecurity budget based on institution structure
Board should review management's determination of cybersecurity preparedness alignment with cyber risks
Board should establish cybersecurity risk ownership and management accountability
Board should approve and continuously review cybersecurity strategy, governance charter, policy and framework
Board should ensure cybersecurity policy applies to all operating entities including subsidiaries
Board should review implementation of cybersecurity framework at least once every 12 months
Board should incorporate cybersecurity as a standard agenda in board meetings
Senior Management should implement board-approved cybersecurity strategy
Senior Management should understand cyber organizational scope and identify cyber threats
Senior Management should ensure mitigation and recovery procedures for cyber risk incidents
Senior Management should continuously improve collection and analysis of cybercrime information
Senior Management should oversee deployment of strong authentication measures
Senior Management should ensure provision of skilled staff for cybersecurity with enhanced background checks
Senior Management should ensure timely reporting to the board on cyber risk status
Senior Management should establish cybersecurity benchmarking framework with board endorsement
Senior Management should incorporate cybersecurity as a standard agenda in senior management meetings
Institutions should provide regular reports of cybersecurity posture to the board
Institutions should document cybersecurity incident response plan with roles, responsibilities, incident detection and assessment
Institutions should collaborate with other institutions and security agencies to share cyber threats
Institutions should create post-incident analysis framework for corrective actions
Institutions should oversee evaluation and management of risks introduced by third-party service providers
CISO should oversee implementation of cybersecurity program and enforce cybersecurity policy
CISO should ensure institution maintains current enterprise-wide knowledge base of users, devices, applications
CISO should ensure information systems meet institution's needs and ICT strategy
CISO should design cybersecurity controls with consideration of users at all levels
CISO should organize professional cyber-related trainings to improve staff technical proficiency
CISO should ensure regular and comprehensive cyber risk assessments
CISO should ensure processes for monitoring IT systems to detect cybersecurity events
CISO should report to CEO on cybersecurity assessment, exceptions, effectiveness of program, and material events
CISO should ensure timely update of incident response mechanism and Business Continuity Plan
CISO should incorporate scenario analysis to consider material cyber-attacks
CISO should ensure frequent data backups of critical IT systems to separate storage locations
CISO should ensure roles and responsibilities for managing cyber risks are clearly defined
CISO should continuously test disaster recovery and Business Continuity Plans
Institutions should engage external consultants with sufficient cybersecurity expertise
Institutions should carry out independent cyber threat test at least once a year
Institutions should incorporate qualified ICT auditors within Internal Audit team
Internal ICT auditors should continuously review and report on cyber risks and controls
Internal ICT auditors should assess design and effectiveness of cybersecurity framework
Internal ICT auditors should conduct regular independent threat and vulnerability assessments
Internal ICT auditors should report to board findings of assessments
Internal ICT auditors should conduct comprehensive penetration tests
Risk management function should assess cybersecurity risks and determine alignment with risk appetite
Risk management function should monitor current and emerging cybersecurity risks
Risk management function should collaborate with system administrators to ensure appropriate control design
Risk management function should maintain comprehensive cyber risk registers
Risk management function should ensure implementation of cyber risk management strategy
Risk management function should safeguard confidentiality, integrity and availability of information
Risk management function should establish comprehensive inventory of IT assets
Risk management function should quantify potential impact by assessing residual cyber risk
Risk management function should report all enterprise risks consistently to the board
Risk management function should conduct red team exercises
External auditors should ensure IT audit scope includes understanding institution's IT infrastructure
External auditors should understand extent of institution's automated controls
External auditors should conduct independent threat and vulnerability assessment
External auditors should conduct comprehensive review of approved cybersecurity strategy
External auditors should conduct comprehensive penetration tests
External auditors should report annually to board and CBK on assessment findings

sections.source_quote: Institutions should carry out an independent cyber threat test at least once a year.

sections.heading: PART III Specific Requirements (cont.)
sections.title: Outsourcing and Third-Party Cybersecurity Requirements
sections.summary: Financial institutions must implement robust governance for outsourcing agreements, including due diligence on service providers, documented contracts, and monitoring of service delivery. They must treat outsourcing agreements as critical infrastructure, select vendors based on compliance and risk assessments, ensure computing resources are secured, and require service providers to comply with all applicable legal and regulatory frameworks. Institutions must also assess third-party risks, perform analytics on their outsourcing portfolio, collaborate with third parties to mitigate risks, and monitor for changes in third parties' business and cyber posture.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates specific cybersecurity controls for third-party relationships, requiring institutions to assess and manage risks from outsourcing agreements to ensure data security during cross-border or international transfers involving third parties.

requirements.text: Have in place adequate governance of outsourcing agreements including due diligence on prospective service providers, documented outsourcing agreements and adequate monitoring of service delivery
Consider all outsourcing agreements as critical infrastructure for regulation and protection for purposes of security of the banking sector and the economy at large
Select their vendors based on compliance and risk assessments
Ensure all computing resources are secured including registrations, licensing, compliance and verification
Ensure all outsourcing contracts require service providers to comply with applicable legal and regulatory frameworks
Understand the inherent risk arising from each third party
Perform analytics on an institution's outsourcing portfolio to understand which pose the most relative risk to an institution
Work collaboratively with third parties to mitigate risks that pose the most risk to an institution
Monitor contracted third parties for changes in their business and cyber posture including expansions, divestitures, breaches and new attacks that may alter the third parties' exposure
Service Level Agreements should have robust provisions in relation to security, service availability, performance metrics or penalties
Develop exit management strategies and contingency plans

sections.source_quote: Have in place adequate governance of outsourcing agreements including due diligence on prospective service providers, documented outsourcing agreements and adequate monitoring of service delivery.
